mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate reduces discount rates and eases financial conditions, which raises equity valuations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is confirmed when rate cuts occur without a simultaneous spike in unemployment or inflation.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 60% of months where FEDFUNDS falls over the prior 3 months and CPIAUCSL does not accelerate sharply.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend indicates weakening household income and slower aggregate demand, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when payroll growth is also soft.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases or if Rank IC stays above -0.03 in any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous acceleration in RSAFS and HOUST signals broad consumer demand and housing-linked economic momentum, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect requires both measures to improve together because retail strength alone is less informative.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-high RSAFS and HOUST condition fails to outperform baseline 60-day SPX returns by at least 2 percentage points on average over any 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO reflects weakening industrial output and margin-sensitive activity, which should pressure earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when GDP growth is also slowing.
failure_condition: Reject if months with falling INDPRO and slower GDPC1 growth still generate positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real rates tighten and equity valuation multiples compress, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal should fail when inflation is stable or falling.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not yield negative 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if average 5-day return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A rising GDPC1 trend indicates sustained real output growth that lifts revenue expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect weakens when labor data deteriorate at the same time.
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 trend periods fail to produce above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, especially when PAYEMS is also rising, over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp slowdown in PAYEMS combined with a rising UNRATE captures labor-market deterioration that weakens consumer spending and earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is invalid if unemployment rises but payrolls continue to expand steadily.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change < 0 and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if the triggered labor-deterioration regime does not generate negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A housing downturn, measured by HOUST falling below its recent average, signals weaker interest-sensitive demand and softer cyclical growth, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect is strongest when retail sales also soften.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if below-average HOUST episodes do not lead to negative 60-day SPX returns relative to baseline, or if the signal flips positive in more than 55% of triggered cases.
